docs.text: TopoTB A software package for calculating the electronic structure and topological properties of tight-binding models. Features Band structure, density of states, Fermi surface, and spin texture Berry phase, Berry curvature, Chern number, and Z₂ number VASP interface for processing band data Wannier90 interface for analyzing wannier90_hr.dat files Batch testing of TB model parameters Citation If you use TopoTB in your research, please cite: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.08615 License Open source. See the LICENSE file in the GitHub repository.

docs.text: API Documentation TopoTB includes the following modules: Module Description TopoTB BrillouinZone2D * 2D Brillouin zone calculations TopoTB FirstBrillouinZone * Brillouin zone handling TopoTB HamiltonianCalculation * Hamiltonian calculations TopoTB TopologicalNumber * Topological invariants (Chern, Z₂) TopoTB Model * Model construction TopoTB Wannier90 * Wannier90 interface TopoTB VASP * VASP interface TopoTB Plots * Plotting utilities Getting help Use ? followed by the function name. Example: ?HSPBand

docs.text: Examples For detailed examples, please refer to: The examples/ folder in the software package The arXiv preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.08615 Main capabilities demonstrated Band structure calculation Berry phase and Chern number calculation Processing VASP output Wannier90 integration Parameter batch testing

docs.text: Installation Method 1: Direct loading Place the TopoTB folder in Mathematica's Applications directory: SystemOpen[FileNameJoin[{$UserBaseDirectory,"Applications"}]] Then copy the TopoTB folder into the opened directory, and load: Needs["TopoTB`"] Method 2: Using Get <<TopoTB` Verify installation FindFile["TopoTB`"]
docs.title: Installation
